items.q: Which spectral type is the hottest?
items.why: The sequence O B A F G K M runs from hottest (O) to coolest (M).

items.q: What causes the absorption lines in a star's spectrum?
items.correct: Cooler gas in the star's atmosphere absorbing specific wavelengths of light.
items.misconception: The star emitting only those few colours.
items.why: Atoms in the outer layers absorb characteristic wavelengths, leaving dark lines in the spectrum.

items.q: On the HR diagram, where do most stars lie?
items.correct: Along the main sequence, a diagonal band.
items.random_wrong: All bunched in the bottom-left corner.
items.why: Most stars spend their lives fusing hydrogen on the main sequence, forming that diagonal band.
Correct answer: G2. “A0” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Approximate surface temperature in Kelvin above which a star is type O?
items.why: The real figure is 30000. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
